name: verified-algebra-workflow
old_string:
## HONESTY BOUNDARY â€” what you CANNOT recover
The DB stores **tool confirmations and compaction summaries, NOT the raw script
source body** of a deleted `write_file`. In this project the 251-line
`hermes-verify-general-fts-state.py` left only a `bytes_written` confirmation and
a diff fragment in the DB; the full source and the shared frame `Q` were NOT in
the archive. Consequence:

> A chat DB can prove a script *existed and ran*. It does **not** reconstruct it.
> Do NOT tell the user "I recovered the script from the database" if all you
> found is a `resolved_path` confirmation. State precisely: "confirmed written to
> Temp on <date>, <N> lines, now deleted; source not recoverable from the archive."

If the raw source IS present (some `write_file` assistant messages embed the full
text), extract it and re-commit it to the project dir â€” never leave it in Temp.
